evals.name: readiness-gaps
evals.prompt: Which workloads in our fleet run as root or have no memory limit?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-workload preflight` then `cub-workload readiness` (JSON or -o table, optionally --failing-only or --dimension security/resources) and reports the workloads whose SECURITY / RESOURCES dimensions warn or fail. Does not mutate, does not fall back to kubectl.

evals.name: fleet-inventory
evals.prompt: Give me a per-cluster count of workloads.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-workload snapshot` (optionally -o table or jq on the JSON) and reports per-cluster workload counts plus totals.

evals.name: score-one-namespace
evals.prompt: How production-ready are the workloads in the apptique namespace on dev-cluster?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-workload readiness --cluster dev-cluster --namespace apptique` and reports each workload's per-dimension pass/warn/fail. Read-only.

evals.name: routes-availability-elsewhere
evals.prompt: Which of our multi-replica workloads have no PodDisruptionBudget?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is a PDB-coverage question, not the readiness scorecard, and routes to workload-availability / `cub-workload availability` rather than answering with snapshot/readiness.
